question: Does the two-teacher failure-patching tier (main teacher + a stronger auxiliary teacher on the failed subset, plus mined DPO pairs) produce a student adapter with higher held-out passAt1 than single-teacher distillation at an equal kept-row budget?
rationale: T1/T7/T9 in agi-proof/gemma4-distillation-research-note.md. The one genuinely new lever vs the prior single-teacher tools/distill_export.py: failures are re-answered by a stronger teacher, re-verified through the shared outcome oracle, kept as patched_after_failure rows, and mined as {prompt,chosen,rejected} DPO pairs (debug-and-recover signal). Self-consistency (--self-consistency-n) is the teacher-free fallback arm.
arms: A_single: single-teacher verifier-gated distillation (baseline; tools/distill_export.py, no --patch-provider)
B_patch: A_single + two-teacher patch tier (--patch-provider <stronger>) + DPO pairs
C_selfconsistency: A_single + verified self-consistency on failures (--self-consistency-n N), no stronger teacher
primaryMetric: student adapter held-out passAt1 (B_patch - A_single), and (C_selfconsistency - A_single), at equal kept-row budget

decontam.rule: every kept teacher trace (incl. patched + DPO) is disjoint from the held-out eval (exact + content-shingle); enforced independently

provenance.rule: every distilled row carries a valid metadata.verification_provenance tag

patchYield.rule: B_patch must actually recover failures (patched > 0) and mine pairs (dpoPairs > 0), else the arm is null by construction

judge.rule: mean adapter winrate > 0.5 across >= 2 families with reported inter-judge agreement (Cohen kappa >= 0.40 OR Gwet AC1 + CI)

stoppingRule: anytime-valid confidence sequence (tools/eval_stats.confidence_sequence_mean) — the distil -> re-judge -> resample loop is optional stopping; fixed-n CI is invalid
decontam: tools/assert_decontam.py over the committed distilled packs + held-out eval; private held-out split remains the open upgrade
ranking: guardrails first (decontam + provenance + judge), then primary passAt1 strength; the simplest arm (A_single) is the baseline that B/C must beat
honestLimits: requires teacher API access (OpenRouter/Anthropic/Zhipu) — egress-gated; not runnable in an air-gapped session
the DPO arm is optional; SFT-only B_patch is the minimal test
deterministic markers partly reward trained FORMAT — the >= 2-family judge is the semantic cross-check
a NULL/NEGATIVE (patch tier does not beat single-teacher) is a valid outcome, like the committed SFT>ORPO negative

runbook.distill_patch: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... tools/distill_export.py prompts.json --provider deepseek --patch-provider glm:glm-5.2 --out-dir training/distill_patch

enforcement.claim_gate: tools/claim_gate.py --prefix distill-patch --assert-prereg (power, uncertainty, anytime-valid, >=2 judge families, decontam, magnitude)
enforcement.oracle_parity: tests/test_outcome_oracle.py (the SFT filter and the RL reward share one definition of correct)

outcomeNote: A NO-GO is a valid publishable outcome — log measured passAt1 deltas + CIs, do not tune teacher prompts or the jaccard threshold to the gate.
